methods.description: Process of increasing the dataset size by applying augmentation techniques to address the shortage of original data, which is advantageous for training a deep learning model.

steps.description: After image segmentation, we obtained 169 images of chironomid larvae, 128 images of yellow worms, and 94 images of mosquito larvae.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain segmented images of different larvae types

steps.description: Create new data by applying augmentation techniques to the four-times dataset, involving random rotations, horizontal and vertical shifts, horizontal flipping, and zooming in and out.
steps.searchable_summary: apply data augmentation techniques to increase dataset size

methods.description: Evaluation of deep learning models using accuracy, loss functions, mAP, confusion matrices, RMSE, and speed to identify the fastest and most accurate algorithm for real-time larval detection.

steps.description: Evaluate the performance of the deep learning models using the accuracy and loss functions.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate model performance using accuracy and loss functions

steps.description: Compute confusion matrices by comparing the actual and predicted label values.
steps.searchable_summary: compute confusion matrices for model evaluation

steps.description: Use mAP as a metric in object detection tasks to assess a model's ability to detect and localize objects across various classes within an image.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mAP for object detection model evaluation

steps.description: Use RMSE and speed as evaluation metrics for model selection.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate models using RMSE and speed metrics

steps.description: Determine the speed of CNN and Resnet101 by measuring the time interval between the start and end of the training process.
steps.searchable_summary: measure training time for CNN and Resnet101

steps.description: Calculate the speed of YOLOv7 by converting the time value generated after training into seconds.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate YOLOv7 training speed in seconds
